jokes.text: That's a good story, Grandpa.
jokes.setup: Someone makes a boring observation about Christmas lights
jokes.punchline: Monica sarcastically calls them 'Grandpa' for the old-fashioned comment
jokes.explanation: Clean, economical putdown with good timing. Classic Monica snark.

jokes.text: Did someone get her drunk again, or?
jokes.setup: Monica is unusually letting others help decorate her tree
jokes.punchline: Implies Monica only abandons control when intoxicated
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific joke that builds on established Monica traits

jokes.text: And we're done. Ta-da!
jokes.setup: Monica gives speech about Christmas being about loved ones, not perfect trees
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts herself by perfectionist 'ta-da' presentation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - says one thing, does another. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: See, now it feels like Christmas.
jokes.setup: Ross adjusts something on the tree after Monica declares it perfect
jokes.punchline: His small change somehow makes it 'feel like Christmas'
jokes.explanation: Mild visual gag about tree perfectionism. Functional but not standout.

jokes.text: So around the same time that you started telling this story.
jokes.setup: Ross mentions Christmas trees date back 4000 years to ancient Egypt
jokes.punchline: Chandler implies Ross has been talking for 4000 years
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback structure using Ross's own fact against him. Classic Chandler timing and delivery.

jokes.text: I can see why that's hard to resist.
jokes.setup: Joey demonstrates Janine's stretching poses that are driving him crazy
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically comments on the 'irresistible' awkward stretching
jokes.explanation: Good visual setup with Chandler's trademark sarcastic commentary

jokes.text: I just want to feed her grapes and brush her hair.
jokes.setup: Joey expressing how sweet Janine is
jokes.punchline: Bizarre, overly intimate activities like she's a pet or deity
jokes.explanation: Perfectly weird Joey line. Specific, unexpected imagery that fits his character.

jokes.text: You are aware that she's not a monkey, right?
jokes.setup: Joey's grape-feeding and hair-brushing comment
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out this sounds like caring for a monkey
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline to Joey's absurd setup. Economical and memorable.

jokes.text: Yeah, at first I thought she was hot, but now she's like old news.
jokes.setup: Joey claiming he's over Janine
jokes.punchline: Transparent attempt to sound casual after his grape-feeding confession
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey self-deception. Works off previous setup effectively.

jokes.text: It's called some sort of Dickin' Rockin' Dicky Eve.
jokes.setup: Janine trying to remember the name of Dick Clark's show
jokes.punchline: Hilariously mangled version focusing on 'Dick'
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism that sounds unintentionally dirty. Character-appropriate confusion.

jokes.text: You're still just a little fat girl inside, aren't you?
jokes.setup: Monica getting excited about Dick Clark's show
jokes.punchline: Janine's blunt observation about Monica's enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: References Monica's backstory. Somewhat mean but character-revealing.

jokes.text: What are you laughing at? Well, I used to date him, but you're still going out with her.
jokes.setup: Joey laughing at something
jokes.punchline: Monica points out Joey is in the more embarrassing situation
jokes.explanation: Good comeback that turns the tables. Monica defending herself effectively.

jokes.text: If she says, 'What are you doing?' I can say, 'It wasn't me, it was New Year's.'
jokes.setup: Joey planning to kiss Janine at midnight
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous excuse blaming the holiday
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - absurd but delivered with complete sincerity. Very quotable.

jokes.text: That's better than Ross kissing me in high school and saying that he did it because he needed Chap Stick. It was a dry day.
jokes.setup: Joey's New Year's excuse being compared to past excuses
jokes.punchline: Ross's pathetic Chap Stick excuse and his defensive response
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with perfect escalation. Ross's defensive 'It was a dry day' is beautifully pathetic.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! It's just like I dreamed it!
jokes.setup: Ross arriving at the TV set after acting cool
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandons coolness with embarrassing enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross geekout moment. Good character consistency with his TV obsession.

jokes.text: I can save you time, ladies. I'm right here.
jokes.setup: Rachel and Phoebe looking for presents
jokes.punchline: Chandler jokes he's Monica's present
jokes.explanation: Standard romantic comedy joke. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Oh, well, that makes it not terrible.
jokes.setup: Learning they search for presents every year
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about tradition not making it better
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler sarcasm with clear structure. Economical delivery.

jokes.text: She's always bested us, that wily... minx.
jokes.setup: Describing Monica's skill at hiding presents
jokes.punchline: Pausing to find the word 'minx' - unexpectedly old-fashioned
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay joke. The pause before 'minx' creates the humor.

jokes.text: Why would you do that to her? Why? Why?
jokes.setup: Arguing Chandler should help find presents
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dramatic guilt trip
jokes.explanation: Overwrought guilt trip. Escalation works but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Dear Losers, do you really think I'd hide presents under the couch? P.S. Chandler, I knew they'd break you.
jokes.setup: Finding what they think is a present
jokes.punchline: Monica's note revealing she anticipated everything, especially Chandler caving
jokes.explanation: Excellent Monica moment showing her control-freak nature. The P.S. about Chandler is perfectly character-specific.

jokes.text: No. Never do that.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking if they search his closet
jokes.punchline: Overly emphatic denial revealing they absolutely do that
jokes.explanation: Good 'lady doth protest too much' moment. The emphatic denial gives it away.

jokes.text: Okay, that did not just happen.
jokes.setup: Accidentally revealing where Joey's stuff is hidden
jokes.punchline: Chandler's attempt to un-say what he just said
jokes.explanation: Standard 'oops I said too much' reaction. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: When you learned to dance, did you forget how to put on underpants?
jokes.setup: Dancers on platforms showing off
jokes.punchline: Ross's judgment about their lack of underwear
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with nice structure linking dancing skill to underwear choices.

jokes.text: Really, you think so? I don't.
jokes.setup: Guy asking to dance with Janine because he thinks he has a shot
jokes.punchline: Ross bluntly disagreeing with the assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery. Economical and brutally honest in classic Ross fashion.

jokes.text: Those are mine. I got those for you.
jokes.setup: Monica calling the presents 'crap'
jokes.punchline: Chandler revealing he bought those 'crap' presents
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward moment. The economy of the reveal makes it hit hard.

jokes.text: Oh. Okay, I'm sorry. Thank you for my 'az.'
jokes.setup: Learning the metal A-Z thing are bookends
jokes.punchline: Monica awkwardly mispronouncing/mishearing as 'az'
jokes.explanation: Good awkward recovery moment. The mispronunciation adds to the discomfort.

jokes.text: Would it help if I weren't wearing underpants? Would it?
jokes.setup: Ross desperately trying to get on the platform
jokes.punchline: Offering to remove underwear like the other dancers
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ross's desperation. Callbacks to earlier underwear joke effectively.

jokes.text: She's nice, huh? To think I almost brought my wife to this.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to talk about Janine
jokes.punchline: Guy reveals he's married and considering cheating
jokes.explanation: Good reveal of sleazy character. Creates uncomfortable comedy through moral issues.

jokes.text: Hey, man. You're the one who wet his pants.
jokes.setup: Guy asking if Joey is in second grade after Joey splashes him
jokes.punchline: Pointing out Joey is the one with wet pants
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy payoff. Functional but relies on obvious slapstick.

jokes.text: Mom and Dad are gonna be so faced!
jokes.setup: Monica excited about doing the routine on TV
jokes.punchline: Using 'faced' instead of a more appropriate word
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay. Monica's excitement coming through awkward slang choice.

jokes.text: So do we really need to ask who's going up on the platform next?
jokes.setup: Monica and Ross doing their elaborate routine
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about their 'obvious' talent
jokes.explanation: Sets up the misdirection. Audience thinks they're good, but tone suggests otherwise.

jokes.text: Get this. Dick will want it for the Bloopers show.
jokes.setup: Seeming to praise Monica and Ross's routine
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's so bad it belongs on a bloopers show
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection. Perfect structure that completely subverts expectations about their 'talent.'

jokes.text: What's the matter with you? Go! Yeah, take a hike, wet pants!
jokes.setup: Joey being blamed for wetting his pants
jokes.punchline: Getting kicked out with crowd joining the mockery
jokes.explanation: Pile-on humor. Works for escalation but relies heavily on embarrassment.

jokes.text: Happy No Year!
jokes.setup: New Year's countdown being interrupted
jokes.punchline: Joey's confused 'No Year' instead of 'New Year'
jokes.explanation: Cute wordplay that captures Joey's confusion. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Yes, if the presents are hidden south for the winter.
jokes.setup: Phoebe suggesting birds can help find presents with their direction sense
jokes.punchline: Chandler pointing out birds migrate south, not find hidden objects
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay mixing migration with present-hunting. Good Chandler-style logical takedown.

jokes.text: Or we could just follow your clever jokes. Any ideas? Didn't think so.
jokes.setup: Chandler's sarcastic bird comment
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic comeback challenging him to help instead
jokes.explanation: Standard comeback. Escalates the banter but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Hmm. How about my 'az'?
jokes.setup: Wondering what to get Monica
jokes.punchline: Referencing Monica's earlier mispronunciation of his gift
jokes.explanation: Good callback that builds on earlier awkwardness. Self-deprecating humor from Chandler.

jokes.text: Don't look directly at them!
jokes.setup: Finding the actual presents
jokes.punchline: Treating presents like dangerous objects not to be looked at
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe weirdness. Treating presents like they're the Ark of the Covenant.

jokes.text: Whatever, Linus. I'm opening mine.
jokes.setup: Chandler making a speech about Christmas spirit
jokes.punchline: Rachel calling him Linus from Peanuts and ignoring the sentiment
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that fits Rachel's dismissive attitude. Economical putdown.

jokes.text: Do you know how long it took me to find you that water purifier? That's what you got me? Yes, I see. That look is priceless.
jokes.setup: Monica defending her gift-giving effort
jokes.punchline: Chandler's visible disappointment being exactly what Monica wanted to avoid
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the gift-hunting plot. Monica's ironic commentary on getting exactly what she feared.

jokes.text: What was that for? Tonight when they yelled 'cut' and we didn't get to kiss... I was really, really disappointed, and I just... I just really wanted to kiss you.
jokes.setup: Joey kissing Janine unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: Janine revealing she wanted to kiss him too, making his scheme work accidentally
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Joey's storyline. His plan works for completely different reasons than expected.

jokes.text: Sure. New Year's Eve is only two weeks away. Can you wait? No. Me neither.
jokes.setup: Joey asking if she wants to kiss again
jokes.punchline: Janine suggesting waiting until actual New Year's, then immediately changing her mind
jokes.explanation: Good structure with quick reversal. Shows mutual attraction developing.

jokes.text: You don't have to count down every time we kiss. Oh. Okay. Except I sort of felt like I needed a couple seconds to get ready.
jokes.setup: Joey starting another countdown
jokes.punchline: Joey's neurotic need for countdown preparation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's quirky thought process. Nice callback to the countdown motif.

jokes.text: Hey, by the way, quick thinking about catching me.
jokes.setup: Monica and Ross recounting their routine
jokes.punchline: Implies Ross failed to catch Monica during their performance
jokes.explanation: Sarcastic comment revealing more about why their routine was blooper-worthy.

jokes.text: I don't know. You were a lot bigger... I mean, stronger back then.
jokes.setup: Discussing whether they could still do their routine
jokes.punchline: Rachel's awkward correction from 'bigger' to 'stronger'
jokes.explanation: Classic awkward word choice correction. The pause makes it worse.

jokes.text: I can't do it! Ahh! [ROSS THUDS] Now, you do that, you're on TV.
jokes.setup: Monica and Ross attempting their routine again
jokes.punchline: Complete failure with Ross falling, followed by Chandler's sarcastic comment
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy payoff. Chandler's comment perfectly caps the sequence by referencing the earlier bloopers joke.
